project.name: wingspan
project.description: Wingspan board-game simulator and RL self-play training pipeline — 2-player core-set with 180 birds, 26 bonus cards, 16 round goals

nodes.name: display.py
nodes.summary: Provides rich ANSI-colored text formatting helpers for bird cards, bonus cards, player boards, and game state used by the CLI agent display layer.

nodes.name: format_cost
nodes.summary: Formats a bird's food cost into a human-readable string with food type icons.

nodes.name: format_bird_full
nodes.summary: Formats a full bird card display including power text and indentation for CLI output.

nodes.name: format_played_bird_full
nodes.summary: Formats a played bird's full display including cached food, eggs laid, and tucked cards.

nodes.name: format_can_play
nodes.summary: Shows which birds in hand are playable given the current food pool, enumerating valid payment options.

nodes.name: format_board
nodes.summary: Renders a complete player board overview including counters, habitats, food, bonuses, and round goal status.

nodes.name: format_bonus_with_setup_help
nodes.summary: Formats a bonus card with setup-phase decision guidance showing qualifying bird counts from hand and tray.

nodes.name: _bird_head
nodes.summary: Builds the header line for a bird card display, including name, habitat icons, cost, egg capacity, and nest type.

nodes.name: _bonus_setup_help
nodes.summary: Generates a per-bonus setup help block summarizing qualifying birds across hand, tray, and already-selected cards.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the cards subsystem, re-exporting parsed card data from the schema and parse subpackages.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the card parsing subsystem, re-exporting the full parse pipeline including catalog, fields, loader, matchers, and power parsers.

nodes.name: catalog.py
nodes.summary: Provides stable bird and bonus card catalog accessors (count, index lookup by card ID) backed by the parsed card list.

nodes.name: bird_index
nodes.summary: Returns the stable integer index for a bird card by its card ID, used for encoding.

nodes.name: bonus_index
nodes.summary: Returns the stable integer index for a bonus card by its card ID, used for encoding.

nodes.name: fields.py
nodes.summary: Parses individual bird and bonus card fields from raw JSON records into typed schema objects (habitat, food cost, nest type, bonus thresholds, goal categories).

nodes.name: parse_food_cost
nodes.summary: Parses a bird's food cost from the raw JSON record into a BirdCost Pydantic model.

nodes.name: bonus_categories_for_bird
nodes.summary: Determines which bonus card categories a bird qualifies for, used to build bonus qualifying-count caches.

nodes.name: parse_bonus_thresholds
nodes.summary: Parses a bonus card's scoring threshold table from raw JSON into a list of (count, vp) pairs.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: Loads and caches all bird, bonus, and round-goal card data from JSON source files, providing the singleton parsed card list used throughout the engine.

nodes.name: load_all
nodes.summary: Public entry point that loads all card data, returning a tuple of (birds, bonus_cards, round_goals).

nodes.name: matchers.py
nodes.summary: Contains 40 regex-based matcher functions for brown/white bird power text, each registering a specific EffectKind pattern via the parse registry decorator.
nodes.languageNotes: Each matcher is registered using a @registry.pattern decorator, mapping power text regex to an EffectKind variant.

nodes.name: pink_matchers.py
nodes.summary: Contains 5 regex-based matcher functions for pink (when-activated) bird powers, registered via the parse registry's pink_pattern decorator.

nodes.name: power.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates bird power text parsing, extracting one or more Effect objects from raw power text by normalizing and dispatching to registered matchers.

nodes.name: parse_power
nodes.summary: Entry point for bird power parsing, normalizes raw power text and returns a Power object with parsed effects and reactive flag.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Provides the pattern and pink_pattern decorator-based registry for bird power matchers, mapping regex patterns to EffectKind constructors.

nodes.name: tags.py
nodes.summary: Defines tag constants and a to_int utility for mapping string tags from raw card JSON to integer indices used in encoding.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Core Pydantic data models and StrEnum types for all card domain objects: Habitat, Food, NestType, EffectKind, BirdCost, Effect, Power, Bird, BonusCard, EndRoundGoal, and their raw JSON record counterparts.
nodes.languageNotes: EffectKind is the central taxonomy of all implemented bird power types (60+ variants); BirdCost has rich helper methods for payment logic.

nodes.name: EffectKind
nodes.summary: StrEnum with 60+ variants enumerating every implemented bird power effect type, serving as the central taxonomy for the power dispatcher.

nodes.name: BirdCost
nodes.summary: Pydantic model representing a bird's food cost with methods for computing specific and wild food requirements and checking payment feasibility.

nodes.name: Bird
nodes.summary: Core Pydantic model for a bird card including name, habitats, food cost, nest type, power, wingspan, and point value.

nodes.name: BonusCard
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a bonus card including its scoring category, qualifying criteria, and point threshold table.

nodes.name: BirdRecord
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for raw bird JSON records with a load() factory method that parses it into a Bird domain object.

nodes.name: decisions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the complete Decision/Choice type hierarchy used across the game engine: 41 Choice subclasses and corresponding Decision[C] containers covering every agent decision point from setup through end-of-round.
nodes.languageNotes: ALL_DECISION_CLASSES ordering is stable and append-only; SetupDecision must remain last. Generic Decision[C: Choice] with Protocol-based Agent is the core extensibility contract.

nodes.name: Choice
nodes.summary: Abstract Pydantic base class for all agent choice variants with a display_label method.

nodes.name: Decision
nodes.summary: Generic Pydantic container Decision[C: Choice] pairing a list of offered choices with the active player ID; consumed by the Agent protocol.

nodes.name: SetupChoice
nodes.summary: Choice subclass for the setup phase representing the selection of initial birds, bonus card, and food tokens with a display_label method.

nodes.name: AcceptExchangeDecision
nodes.summary: Decision for optional-then-commit effects; carry the full exchange ledger in the accept choice so agents can evaluate the trade before committing.

nodes.name: DecisionFamily
nodes.summary: Groups related Decision subclasses into a named family for the RL encoder's one-hot decision-type stripe.

nodes.name: choice_encode.py
nodes.summary: Encodes agent choice vectors for every Decision subclass into fixed-size float32 tensors for the RL policy network, including board-impact deltas for scoring and bonus goals.
nodes.languageNotes: encode_choices is the main entry; each _featurize_* function handles one Choice subclass; _fill_* helpers populate bonus/goal delta stripes encoding expected score impact of each choice.

nodes.name: encode_choices
nodes.summary: Main encoding entry point: iterates over all offered choices for a Decision, calling per-type featurizers to fill a (n_choices, choice_dim) tensor.

nodes.name: _featurize_setup
nodes.summary: Featurizes SetupChoice vectors including bird identity, bonus delta, goal delta, and food selection for the setup phase.

nodes.name: _fill_bonus_delta
nodes.summary: Computes and fills the expected bonus VP delta stripe for a choice, accounting for egg, hand, and placement impacts on all active bonus cards.

nodes.name: _fill_goal_delta
nodes.summary: Computes and fills the expected round goal VP delta stripe for a choice, dispatching per goal category.

nodes.name: _fill_board_slots
nodes.summary: Fills the board slots section of a choice vector with habitat occupancy, egg counts, and wingspan values for the landing slot.

nodes.name: actions.py
nodes.summary: Implements the four main Wingspan turn actions (play bird, gain food, lay eggs, draw cards) as free functions taking the Engine as first argument, including helper logic for enumerating playable birds and offering birdfeeder resets.

nodes.name: do_play_bird
nodes.summary: Executes the play-bird action: offers the player a bird selection, collects food payment, places the bird, and activates row powers.

nodes.name: do_gain_food
nodes.summary: Executes the gain-food action by offering birdfeeder dice and resolving conversions from the current board slot.

nodes.name: do_lay_eggs
nodes.summary: Executes the lay-eggs action, computing the number of eggs from the board slot and offering placement choices.

nodes.name: do_draw_cards
nodes.summary: Executes the draw-cards action, offering source choices (tray or deck) for each draw based on the board slot count.

nodes.name: activate_row_powers
nodes.summary: Iterates all birds in the activated habitat row and fires brown/white power handlers for each.

nodes.name: offer_birdfeeder_reset
nodes.summary: Offers the active player a birdfeeder reset decision when the feeder is empty or the player opts to reroll.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Engine class â€” the central game orchestrator that owns the turn loop, setup phase, Agent protocol plumbing, and ask() validation gateway.
nodes.languageNotes: Engine.ask() is the sole agent-interaction gateway enforcing type-safe choice validation. All action logic lives in sibling free-function modules that take Engine as first arg.

nodes.name: Engine
nodes.summary: Core game orchestrator managing the full game lifecycle: setup, turn loop, scoring, and agent interaction via the ask() protocol.

nodes.name: Agent
nodes.summary: Protocol class defining the callable interface for Wingspan agents: accepts a Decision[C] and returns a matching Choice.

nodes.name: helpers.py
nodes.summary: Provides food cost evaluation helpers: cost_meets checks if a food pool satisfies a bird cost, and enumerate_payments generates all valid payment combinations.

nodes.name: enumerate_payments
nodes.summary: Generates all valid food payment combinations that satisfy a bird cost from the given food pool, used to present payment choices to the agent.

nodes.name: any_payment_exists
nodes.summary: Quickly checks whether any valid food payment exists for a bird cost without enumerating all combinations.

nodes.name: log_format.py
nodes.summary: Provides structured log message formatting for game events (setup, dealt hand, turn summaries) emitted by the Engine to the display agent.

nodes.name: log_turn_summary
nodes.summary: Formats and emits a complete end-of-turn summary including score tables, food, and board state.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the engine powers subsystem, re-exporting all bird power handler submodules.

nodes.name: dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Core power dispatching logic: routes an EffectKind to its registered handler, applies effects, offers activation veto, and implements the shared lay_one_egg_on_nest helper used by multiple powers.

nodes.name: dispatch_power
nodes.summary: Routes a bird's EffectKind to its registered handler function in the powers registry.

nodes.name: apply_effect
nodes.summary: Applies a single bird Effect by dispatching to the appropriate handler, handling UNIMPLEMENTED fallback gracefully.

nodes.name: lay_one_egg_on_nest
nodes.summary: Shared helper that offers a player an egg placement on a specific nest type, used by multiple power handlers.

nodes.name: drafting.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for bird-drafting effects: play an additional bird here, draw-N-plus-one-draft, and draw-bonus-keep mechanics.

nodes.name: egg_trade.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for egg-trading effects: discard an egg to gain wild food or draw cards.

nodes.name: grants.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for the largest group of bird power effects: food gain (supply, feeder, birdfeeder), egg laying, card drawing, caching, tucking, and combination tuck-then effects.

nodes.name: multi_actor.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for effects involving multiple players: each-player-gains-a-die-in-order and all-players-lay-egg-on-nest.

nodes.name: nest_aggregate.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for nest-aggregation effects: lay an egg on every bird of a given nest type, gain all food of a type from the feeder, and tuck from deck with payment.

nodes.name: predator_repeat.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for predator-hunt, move-bird-if-rightmost, repeat-brown-power, and repeat-predator-power effects.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Provides the @handles decorator registry that maps EffectKind variants to power handler functions, and handler_for lookup used by dispatch.

nodes.name: tray_trade.py
nodes.summary: Power handlers for tray-related effects: draw all from tray, trade wild food, and fewest-habitat conditional gains (die or card).

nodes.name: reactors.py
nodes.summary: Implements pink power reactor triggers: fires when-activated reactions from all opponent birds for lay-egg, predator success, play-bird, and gain-food events.

nodes.name: trigger_pink_lay_eggs_reactors
nodes.summary: Triggers all pink lay-egg reactors on the opponent's board when the active player lays eggs.

nodes.name: fire_pink_lay_egg
nodes.summary: Executes a single pink lay-egg reactor, offering the opponent egg placement with veto, handling all nest/habitat match variants.

nodes.name: trigger_pink_predator_success
nodes.summary: Triggers pink predator-success reactors on both players when a predator hunt succeeds.

nodes.name: scoring.py
nodes.summary: Implements all Wingspan scoring logic: round goal evaluation and standing, bonus card qualifying counts and VP, final scoring, and per-choice score delta computation for the RL encoder.

nodes.name: final_scoring
nodes.summary: Computes final game scores for all players including bird VP, egg VP, cached food VP, tucked cards, round goal VP, and bonus card VP.

nodes.name: score_round_goal
nodes.summary: Evaluates a round goal for all players and returns scored VP per player based on standings.

nodes.name: bonus_score
nodes.summary: Returns the VP score for a player's bonus card given the current qualifying count.

nodes.name: goal_count_delta_for_bird
nodes.summary: Computes the change in a round goal's qualifying count when a specific bird is added to a habitat.

nodes.name: _best_case_egg_count_delta
nodes.summary: Estimates best-case egg count delta for a round goal given optimal egg placement, used by the RL encoder.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Defines all mutable game state models: GameState (full game), Player, Board, PlayedBird, Birdfeeder, FoodPool, and RoundGoalResult â€” the core data layer for the simulation.
nodes.languageNotes: Birdfeeder and FoodPool have rich dict-like interfaces; Board encodes the habitat track actions with per-slot conversion logic; GameState.reset_turn_state() is the per-turn scratch reset.

nodes.name: GameState
nodes.summary: Top-level mutable game state Pydantic model holding both players, the shared birdfeeder, tray, round goals, and per-turn scratch state.

nodes.name: Player
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single player's state: hand, board, food pool, bonus cards, round goal results, and per-turn accumulators.

nodes.name: Board
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a player's habitat board with dict-like access by Habitat key, slot counts, and action offer conversion logic.

nodes.name: Birdfeeder
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for the shared birdfeeder with dice-taking, rerolling, gain-option enumeration, and formatting logic.

nodes.name: FoodPool
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a player's food token pool with full dict-like interface over Food enum keys.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Top-level package initializer for the wingspan simulator; currently minimal (2 non-empty lines) but marks the root package boundary.

nodes.name: architecture.py
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical neural network topology descriptor `ModelArchitecture` and supporting parameter-counting utilities (`count_parameters`, `ParamReport`) used by `PolicyValueNet` and the HTML report to describe and size every model block.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic models (`ModelArchitecture`, `LayerParam`, `BlockParam`, `ParamReport`) for all architecture configuration; `ActivationName` is a `StrEnum`.

nodes.name: ModelArchitecture
nodes.summary: Pydantic model that is the single source of truth for all neural network topology hyperparameters â€” layer widths, activation, dropout, card embedding dim, hand encoder config, and tray set embedding mode.

nodes.name: ParamReport
nodes.summary: Aggregates parameter counts broken out by model block (embed, trunk, choice, scorer, value, hand) for display in the HTML architecture report.

nodes.name: count_parameters
nodes.summary: Computes a detailed `ParamReport` by summing parameter counts for each model block given an architecture descriptor and input dimensions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the backward-compatibility shim layer; imports from `v0_0` and `v0_1` to expose version-routing helpers at the `compat` namespace level.

nodes.name: v0_0.py
nodes.summary: Backward-compatibility shim for artifact version 0.0: provides the legacy choice encoding layout, `PolicyValueNetV00` subclass that uses the old flat choice encoder, and reconstruction helpers that translate old encoded tensors to the current layout.
nodes.languageNotes: Reconstructs the old v0.0 choice stripe layout from scratch using private helpers so that old checkpoint tensors can be loaded and adapted to the live model.

nodes.name: PolicyValueNetV00
nodes.summary: Subclass of PolicyValueNet that overrides choice encoding and embedding to use the v0.0 flat encoder layout, enabling old checkpoints to be loaded and evaluated.

nodes.name: v0_1.py
nodes.summary: Backward-compatibility shim for artifact version 0.1: provides the legacy card-feature matrix layout (without current bonus embeddings) and `PolicyValueNetV01` / `SetupNetV01` subclasses that override the card encoder to use the old feature matrix.

nodes.name: PolicyValueNetV01
nodes.summary: Overrides `_build_card_encoder` to use the v0.1 card feature matrix (fixed-size feature rows from a pre-computed buffer) rather than the current learned embedding.

nodes.name: SetupNetV01
nodes.summary: Mirrors `PolicyValueNetV01` for the setup model: overrides `_build_card_encoder` to register the v0.1 card feature buffers.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer and public API facade for the encoder subsystem; re-exports `encode_state`, `encode_choices`, `card_feature_matrix`, `card_summary_matrix`, `trunk_input_dim`, `choice_input_dim`, `EncodingSpec`, `state_size`, and `choice_feature_dim` from submodules.

nodes.name: layout.py
nodes.summary: Defines `EncodingSpec` (the selector between main-game and setup encoding) and computes all top-level dimension constants â€” trunk input, choice input, choice feature, state feature, decision-type, and family counts â€” that wire together the stripe layout and the neural network input layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the central dimension registry; downstream modules query `layout.trunk_input_dim`, `layout.choice_feature_dim`, etc. rather than computing dims independently.

nodes.name: EncodingSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic model that selects between game-play encoding and setup-phase encoding, controlling which stripe layouts and dimension constants apply.

nodes.name: trunk_input_dim
nodes.summary: Computes the total trunk input width by summing the state stripe dimension after applying card embedding substitutions, hand encoder output, and tray set embedding widths.

nodes.name: state_encode.py
nodes.summary: Converts a `GameState` snapshot into a flat float32 numpy array suitable for neural network input, assembling board slots, food, hand, bonus progress, round goals, birdfeeder, and decision-type stripes.
nodes.languageNotes: ~2381-dim output; `encode_state` is the primary entry point, delegating to ~15 private stripe helpers that each handle one semantic region of the state vector.

nodes.name: encode_state
nodes.summary: Top-level state encoder: assembles all game-state stripes (food, board slots, summaries, bonus progress, birdfeeder, round goals, card indices, decision type) into a single concatenated float32 array.

nodes.name: card_feature_matrix
nodes.summary: Builds the static (n_birds Ã— feature_dim) matrix of per-bird attribute vectors, used as the card encoder's input buffer and cached for inference.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the stripes subpackage; re-exports `StripeDescriptor`, `SubFieldDescriptor`, `VectorLayout`, and the four layout functions from sibling modules.

nodes.name: card_feature.py
nodes.summary: Produces `VectorLayout` descriptors for the card-feature stripe (all 180 bird attribute sub-fields) and the hand-encoder input stripe, used by the HTML report and the compat shim to document and validate vector shapes.

nodes.name: choice.py
nodes.summary: Defines the complete choice encoding vector layout: `raw_choice_stripe_layout` emits per-decision-class stripes with sub-field descriptors for kind, gain-food, payment, board-target, exchange, bonus/goal deltas, and setup aggregates; `choice_stripe_layout` wraps it with embedding substitution.

nodes.name: raw_choice_stripe_layout
nodes.summary: Builds the complete per-decision-family choice stripe layout, iterating over all active decision classes and emitting a `StripeDescriptor` with detailed sub-fields for each semantic region of the choice vector.

nodes.name: descriptors.py
nodes.summary: Defines the three Pydantic descriptor types â€” `SubFieldDescriptor`, `StripeDescriptor`, and `VectorLayout` â€” that form the self-documenting layer layout schema used by the HTML report and compat shims.

nodes.name: StripeDescriptor
nodes.summary: Describes one named stripe within a vector layout, including its byte offset, size, encoding type, value range, and ordered list of sub-fields.

nodes.name: VectorLayout
nodes.summary: Container for the full ordered sequence of `StripeDescriptor` objects and the total vector size, representing the complete layout of a state or choice tensor.

nodes.name: embed_rules.py
nodes.summary: Provides `_EmbedRule` descriptor and the four rule-set factory functions (`state_embed_rules`, `setup_embed_rules`, `choice_embed_rules`) plus `embed_layout` which applies rules to a raw `VectorLayout`, replacing card-index stripes with embedding-dimension placeholders for the network's actual input.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Defines `state_stripe_layout`, the authoritative `VectorLayout` descriptor for the full game-state tensor covering food, board slots, board summaries, hand, bonus progress, birdfeeder, misc scalars, round goals, card indices, and decision-type stripes.

nodes.name: state_stripe_layout
nodes.summary: Constructs the complete annotated state vector layout, assembling ~20+ `StripeDescriptor` entries covering every semantic region of the ~2381-dim state encoding.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Defines `PolicyValueNet`, the primary PyTorch model for gameplay: builds a card encoder, optional hand encoder, state trunk, per-family choice scorer, and value head from a `ModelArchitecture` config; exposes `encode_state`, `encode_choices`, and `forward` for the training/inference pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Card encoder runs in eval mode during training (frozen weights); a cached `card_table()` matrix is invalidated on train/eval mode switch. Compat shims subclass this to override `_build_card_encoder` or `encode_choices`.

nodes.name: PolicyValueNet
nodes.summary: Main actor-critic network: encodes game state through a card encoder + trunk, encodes each choice through a choice encoder, then scores per decision-family logits and a scalar value estimate.

nodes.name: hand_model.py
nodes.summary: Provides four tensor-manipulation helpers (`multihot_from_indices`, `set_summary_from_indices`, `set_summary_from_multihot`, `embed_card_set`) for converting card index tensors into multi-hot or summary-row representations fed into the hand encoder.

nodes.name: mlp.py
nodes.summary: Provides `build_body` and `build_readout` factory functions that construct `nn.Sequential` MLP blocks from layer width lists, activation, dropout, and LayerNorm settings, used by all neural network components in the project.

nodes.name: build_body
nodes.summary: Constructs a multi-layer perceptron body as `nn.Sequential`, supporting configurable activation (via `ActivationName`), optional LayerNorm, and optional dropout between layers.

nodes.name: html.py
nodes.summary: Generates a self-contained HTML architecture report page from the model's encoding layouts, architecture config, and parameter count report, including stripe tables, architecture diagram (embedded SVG), and parameter breakdown.

nodes.name: generate_html_report
nodes.summary: Entry point for HTML report generation: assembles architecture section (SVG diagram), state/choice vector sections (stripe tables), card-feature and hand-encoder sections, and parameter breakdown into a full HTML page.

nodes.name: svg.py
nodes.summary: Renders the neural-network architecture as an inline SVG diagram with labeled blocks, op rows (Linear/LayerNorm/Activation/Dropout), IO boxes, connector bands between rows, and parameter count badges; entry point is `build_arch_svg`.
nodes.languageNotes: Entirely string-template based SVG generation with no external SVG library; uses private Pydantic models (`_Unit`, `_Conn`, `_Geom`, `_OpRow`) to describe the diagram geometry before rendering.

nodes.name: build_arch_svg
nodes.summary: Top-level SVG builder: assembles architecture units, resolves geometry, draws units and connectors, and concatenates all SVG fragments into the final inline string.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer and public API barrel for the setup model subsystem; re-exports `SetupEncoding`, `SetupArchitecture`, `setup_readout_input_dim`, `count_setup_parameters`, `SetupNet`, `generate`, and `record` from submodules.

nodes.name: architecture.py
nodes.summary: Defines `SetupEncoding` (the feature vector structure for game-setup candidates) and `SetupArchitecture` (the neural network config for the setup net), plus `count_setup_parameters` for parameter reporting.

nodes.name: SetupEncoding
nodes.summary: Pydantic model that describes the dimensionality and layout of the setup-phase candidate feature vector, with properties computing offsets and sizes for each semantic region (kept cards, food, tray, feeder, goals, bonus values).

nodes.name: SetupArchitecture
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for setup network hyperparameters: hidden layer widths, activation function, dropout, and whether to use a policy head (actor-critic mode).

nodes.name: candidates.py
nodes.summary: Defines `SetupCandidate` (the typed representation of one possible initial-setup selection) and `enumerate_setup_candidates` / `select_by_margins` for generating and sampling from the combinatorial space of card+food+bonus choices.

nodes.name: SetupCandidate
nodes.summary: Pydantic model representing one candidate initial setup: which bird cards to keep, which food tokens to take, and which bonus card to select.

nodes.name: encode.py
nodes.summary: Converts a `SetupCandidate` and game context into a fixed-length float32 feature vector for the setup net, encoding kept cards, food, bonus affinity, tray indices, birdfeeder state, and round-goal affinity stripes.

nodes.name: SetupContext
nodes.summary: Snapshot of the shared game context needed to encode a setup candidate: tray birds, birdfeeder counts, round goal categories, and dealt bonus cards.

nodes.name: encode_setup_candidate
nodes.summary: Fills a zero-initialized float32 array with the encoded representation of a setup candidate, writing kept-card indices, food counts, bonus block, tray indices, birdfeeder, round goals, bonus values, and goal affinity.

nodes.name: generate.py
nodes.summary: Provides `RandomSetupGenerator`, which samples training-data setup instances by randomly dealing and selecting initial cards and food, using softmax-weighted sampling to create realistic and diverse training data.

nodes.name: RandomSetupGenerator
nodes.summary: Generates randomized setup instances for training data collection, dealing cards and food at random, scoring food options via bird compatibility, and sampling diverse candidate selections.

nodes.name: record.py
nodes.summary: Provides `SetupSample` (training record with features, margin, and candidate array) and `SetupDataStore` (append-only JSONL file store with iteration, count, and clear operations) for setup model training data persistence.

nodes.name: SetupDataStore
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL file store for setup training samples, supporting iteration, sample count, and file-level clearing; serializes `_SetupRow` records via Pydantic's `model_dump_json`.

nodes.name: stripes.py
nodes.summary: Produces annotated `VectorLayout` descriptors for the setup candidate input vector (`setup_stripe_layout`) and the setup-net readout input after embedding substitution (`setup_readout_stripe_layout`), mirroring the structure of `encode/stripes/state.py` for the setup domain.

nodes.name: setup_learner.py
nodes.summary: Implements offline (batch-MSE) and online (per-game actor-critic) training update functions for the setup net: `offline_fit` trains from a stored JSONL dataset, `online_update` from a live sample list, and `actor_critic_update` runs a policy-gradient + value loss update over grouped samples.
nodes.languageNotes: Actor-critic mode groups samples by iteration key and computes a combined policy (log-softmax) + value (MSE) loss per group; gradient clipping is applied after each backward pass.

nodes.name: actor_critic_update
nodes.summary: Performs a single actor-critic gradient update on the setup net: groups samples by iteration, computes policy+value loss per group via `_ac_group_loss`, clips gradients, and returns `SetupUpdateStats`.

nodes.name: setup_net.py
nodes.summary: Defines `SetupNet`, the PyTorch model for predicting setup quality margins: builds a frozen card encoder, an optional hand encoder, and a readout MLP (with optional policy head for actor-critic mode) from `SetupArchitecture` and `ModelArchitecture` configs.

nodes.name: SetupNet
nodes.summary: Neural network for game-setup quality prediction: encodes bird cards via a frozen card encoder, embeds the hand-summary vector via an optional hand encoder, concatenates with tabular features, and outputs a scalar margin (or policy + value pair in AC mode).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the cloud module that re-exports all public symbols from monitor, runfile, runner, s3sync, and status submodules.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the cloud module that parses arguments, loads a CloudRunFile (optionally from S3), applies overrides, and launches HeadlessRunner for a remote training run.

nodes.name: monitor.py
nodes.summary: Live terminal dashboard that polls S3 for RunStatus snapshots from all active cloud training runs and renders them in a Rich table with status LEDs, ETA, and win-rate columns.

nodes.name: runfile.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models and parser for the cloud run configuration file, defining S3Config, SyncConfig, and CloudRunFile structures that drive headless training runs.

nodes.name: runner.py
nodes.summary: HeadlessRunner orchestrates a cloud training session: restores from S3, launches a training thread, supervises progress, periodically uploads checkpoints and game chunks, publishes status snapshots, and handles graceful shutdown on signals.

nodes.name: s3sync.py
nodes.summary: S3Sync class and utility functions for downloading runs, uploading checkpoints and game chunks, fetching text objects, and listing run statuses from an S3 bucket backing cloud training.

nodes.name: status.py
nodes.summary: RunStatus Pydantic model and build_status factory that computes a rich training status snapshot from RunState, including win rate, ETA, games-per-second, and evaluation metrics.

nodes.name: loaders.py
nodes.summary: Checkpoint loading functions for PolicyValueNet and SetupNet, with compat shim routing for v0.0 and v0.1 artifact formats, encoding-key validation, and net reconstruction from run-dir metadata.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the reporting module that re-exports the inspect_cli entry point.

nodes.name: inspect_cli.py
nodes.summary: Rich terminal CLI for inspecting trained model checkpoints: prints state-encoding layout, choice-encoding stripes, architecture diagram, parameter counts, and optionally writes an HTML report.

nodes.name: dashboard.py
nodes.summary: Rich-based live terminal dashboard for tournament mode: builds a four-panel layout (header, standings, events, progress) and renders real-time stats from a TournamentLive object.

nodes.name: S3Config
nodes.summary: Pydantic model holding S3 connection parameters: bucket, prefix, region, and optional endpoint URL for MinIO-compatible stores.

nodes.name: SyncConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for sync policy: status poll interval, checkpoint upload frequency, game-chunk thresholds, and whether to restore from S3 on start.

nodes.name: CloudRunFile
nodes.summary: Top-level Pydantic model for a cloud run configuration file, composing S3Config, SyncConfig, and TrainConfig and aligning the training checkpoint directory to the S3 run prefix.

nodes.name: HeadlessRunner
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a full cloud training session: spawns a training thread, supervises it from a main loop that periodically publishes RunStatus to S3, offloads game chunks, handles signals for graceful stop, and performs a final sync on completion.

nodes.name: S3Sync
nodes.summary: Boto3-backed S3 client facade for training artifact I/O: downloads run checkpoints, uploads individual files and byte payloads, uploads checkpoint sets, and offloads game-log chunks with seek-based incremental reads.

nodes.name: RunStatus
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a cloud training run status snapshot, holding iteration progress, win rate, ETA, games-per-second, and metadata fields that are serialized to S3 for the monitor dashboard.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses args, configures logging, loads the run file, creates an S3Sync client, and launches HeadlessRunner.

nodes.name: _apply_overrides
nodes.summary: Applies CLI flag overrides (run name, S3 bucket/prefix/endpoint) to a parsed CloudRunFile by returning a patched copy.

nodes.name: _parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines and parses the cloud CLI argument parser with config, run-name, bucket, prefix, and endpoint arguments.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Monitor CLI entry point: polls S3 for run statuses in a Rich Live loop, rendering a status table on each tick until interrupted.

nodes.name: _render
nodes.summary: Builds a Rich table of run status rows from a list of RunStatus snapshots, with columns for status LED, name, phase, iteration, win rate, games/sec, and ETA.

nodes.name: _parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines and parses monitor CLI args: S3 bucket, prefix, region, endpoint, and poll interval.

nodes.name: _git_sha
nodes.summary: Returns the current git short-SHA by running a subprocess git command, or None if git is unavailable.

nodes.name: iter_run_statuses
nodes.summary: Yields RunStatus objects parsed from all status JSON files found under the configured S3 prefix, used by the monitor to aggregate active runs.

nodes.name: fetch_text
nodes.summary: Fetches and decodes a text object from an S3 URI, used to load run configs stored remotely.

nodes.name: _list_keys
nodes.summary: Paginates S3 list_objects_v2 calls to collect all matching object keys under a given bucket and prefix.

nodes.name: build_status
nodes.summary: Constructs a RunStatus snapshot from the current RunState, computing win rate, ETA, games-per-second, opponent label, and evaluation metrics for the monitor and cloud supervisor.

nodes.name: _strength
nodes.summary: Derives win rate and confidence interval fields from RunState, choosing between EWMA, collection EWMA, and latest eval depending on training phase.

nodes.name: load_policy_net
nodes.summary: Loads a PolicyValueNet from a checkpoint file, routing through compat shims for v0.0 and v0.1 artifact formats, and validating encoding-key compatibility before returning the net in eval mode.

nodes.name: load_policy_net_from_run_dir
nodes.summary: Loads a PolicyValueNet from a run directory by reading the latest checkpoint and reconstructing the network topology from saved run metadata.

nodes.name: load_setup_net
nodes.summary: Loads a SetupNet from a run directory, dispatching between v0.1 and current architecture based on compat flags and returning the net in eval mode on the requested device.

nodes.name: expected_encoding_key
nodes.summary: Computes the canonical encoding key tuple expected for a given run descriptor, reflecting active decision families and current state/choice dimension layout.

nodes.name: _reconstruct_net
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a PolicyValueNet topology from the model config stored in a run directory, validating encoding-key match before instantiating the net.

nodes.name: main_inspect
nodes.summary: Main entry point for the inspect CLI: parses args, loads arch info, and dispatches to state, choice, architecture, and parameter reporting sections, optionally writing an HTML report.

nodes.name: _write_html_report
nodes.summary: Writes a model summary HTML report to a run directory by calling runmeta.build_model_summary_html and saving to an output path.

nodes.name: _make_stripe_table
nodes.summary: Builds a Rich table displaying encoding stripe layout with columns for section, stripe, name, start dimension, size, and description.

nodes.name: _print_arch_section
nodes.summary: Prints the network architecture section: renders the static ASCII arch diagram alongside per-decision-family input dimension and extra-feature counts.

nodes.name: _build_params_table
nodes.summary: Builds a Rich table of parameter counts per network block, with columns for component name, params, percentage, and cumulative totals.

nodes.name: _load_arch_info
nodes.summary: Loads model architecture metadata from a checkpoint directory, returning an _ArchInfo with encoding spec, architecture, and optional setup model architecture.

nodes.name: _print_params_section
nodes.summary: Prints the per-layer/per-block parameter breakdown to the Rich console, era-routed so totals match the run checkpoint.

nodes.name: build_layout
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the root Rich Layout tree for the tournament dashboard with header, standings, events, and progress panels.

nodes.name: _build_standings
nodes.summary: Renders the standings panel with per-agent rows showing win rate, average margin, score sparkline, and games count.

nodes.name: _build_events
nodes.summary: Renders the events panel as a scrolling log of recent tournament game events with color-coded event type glyphs.

nodes.name: _build_progress
nodes.summary: Renders a braille progress bar footer showing completed and remaining game counts for the current tournament.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the training module, re-exporting the main app entry point and serving as the module barrel for consumers like the cloud runner.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Minimal Python __main__ that invokes the training app entry point when the training package is run directly via python -m wingspan.training.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Training application entry point: parses CLI args, builds TrainConfig, launches a TrainingLoop in a worker thread, runs the Rich terminal dashboard, and prints a completion summary.

nodes.name: artifacts.py
nodes.summary: Utility functions for generating canonical checkpoint and final-eval artifact filenames from iteration numbers, used by the training loop for consistent artifact naming.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the training charts subpackage, re-exporting braille canvas, convergence chart, histogram, and inset renderable classes.

nodes.name: braille.py
nodes.summary: BrailleCanvas class for terminal-based dot-matrix plotting using Unicode Braille characters, with a Bresenham line-drawing algorithm for rendering line series.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes pixel dots into Braille Unicode code points (U+2800 block) using a 2x4 dot-per-cell grid; Bresenham's algorithm handles sub-cell interpolation.

nodes.name: convergence_chart.py
nodes.summary: GettingBetterChart Rich renderable that displays a side-by-side Braille convergence chart with win-rate and score/margin plot blocks, axis labels, inset histograms, and phase markers for the training dashboard.

nodes.name: geometry.py
nodes.summary: Chart size constants defining pixel-to-braille-cell dimensions and layout proportions used across convergence and inset chart renderers.

nodes.name: histogram.py
nodes.summary: FamilyHistogram Rich renderable displaying a bar chart of decision-family usage counts for the training dashboard inset, with color-coded tier bars and a total-decisions footer.

nodes.name: BrailleCanvas
nodes.summary: 2D canvas that maps pixel coordinates to Braille Unicode character cells, supporting set_dot, line-drawing via Bresenham, and cell retrieval for terminal chart rendering.

nodes.name: GettingBetterChart
nodes.summary: Rich __rich_console__ renderable composing win-rate and score-margin Braille plot blocks, optional eval insets, and per-iteration axis annotations into the main training dashboard chart.

nodes.name: FamilyHistogram
nodes.summary: Rich renderable that renders a horizontal bar chart of decision-family counts with color-coded tier bars and a total-decisions summary line, used as an inset in the training dashboard.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Training CLI entry point: parses args, optionally runs the interactive GPU configurator, and dispatches to _run_training.

nodes.name: _run_training
nodes.summary: Runs a complete training session: resolves device, configures logging, starts the TrainingLoop in a thread, drives the dashboard UI, joins the worker, prints a summary, and optionally archives the run.

nodes.name: _run_dashboard
nodes.summary: Main UI event loop for the training dashboard: drives a Rich Live display with keyboard input polling, pause/generation-target hotkeys, and stop-on-Escape.

nodes.name: _handle_pause_key
nodes.summary: Processes keyboard events during the training dashboard: digits accumulate a target-generation buffer, space/enter commit it, and other keys reset.

nodes.name: _print_summary
nodes.summary: Prints a post-training summary to the terminal including elapsed time, games-per-second throughput, decision breakdown, and final win rate.

nodes.name: _parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines the full training CLI argument parser with options for device, iterations, architecture layers, learning rate, checkpoint paths, and experimental flags.

nodes.name: _config_from_namespace
nodes.summary: Converts a parsed argparse Namespace into a TrainConfig Pydantic model, parsing layer tuples and activation names from string arguments.

nodes.name: _winrate_block
nodes.summary: Renders the win-rate Braille plot block: draws the EWMA series split above/below the threshold, overlays phase-change markers, and returns labeled grid lines.

nodes.name: _score_margin_block
nodes.summary: Renders the score-margin Braille plot block: draws score and margin EWMA series on a dual-axis grid and returns labeled grid lines.

nodes.name: _render_plot_grid
nodes.summary: Converts a BrailleCanvas into a list of Rich Text lines with left-axis label gutters, beacon highlight, target line, phase markers, and baseline annotation.

nodes.name: _render_dual_axis_grid
nodes.summary: Converts a BrailleCanvas into Rich Text lines with left and right label gutters, a beacon highlight, and a zero-row marker for dual-axis score/margin plots.

nodes.name: _draw_series
nodes.summary: Plots a data series onto a BrailleCanvas by converting (iteration, value) points to canvas coordinates and drawing connected line segments.

nodes.name: _draw_series_split
nodes.summary: Plots a split-color win-rate series onto a BrailleCanvas: segments above the win threshold use one color series and segments below use another, with interpolated crossing points.

nodes.name: final_ckpt_name
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical filename for a final checkpoint artifact at a given iteration number.

nodes.name: final_eval_name
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical filename for a final evaluation artifact at a given iteration number.

nodes.name: insets.py
nodes.summary: Renders rich text inset and strip widgets for the training dashboard, displaying eval and collect phase metrics (win-rate EWMA, margin, game counts) as formatted terminal panels.

nodes.name: eval_inset
nodes.summary: Builds a multi-section rich text inset panel summarizing the latest eval result including win rate, opponent, score margins, and timing.

nodes.name: collect_inset
nodes.summary: Builds a rich text inset panel for the collection phase, showing win-rate EWMA, score margin stats, and game count details.

nodes.name: eval_strip
nodes.summary: Renders a compact single-line strip showing the latest eval win percentage and iteration for use in compact dashboard layouts.

nodes.name: collect_strip
nodes.summary: Renders a compact single-line strip for the collect phase showing win rate and margin EMWAs.

nodes.name: text_helpers.py
nodes.summary: Provides low-level text rendering utilities for terminal chart components: Unicode sparkline generation, eighth-block bar rendering, human-readable number formatting, and multi-column layout joining.

nodes.name: sparkline
nodes.summary: Converts a list of float values into a Unicode sparkline string using block characters, scaled to the given width.

nodes.name: eighth_bar
nodes.summary: Renders a fractional progress bar using Unicode eighth-block characters for sub-cell precision.

nodes.name: join_columns
nodes.summary: Horizontally joins multiple lists of Rich Text rows into a single column-aligned output, padding shorter columns to equal height.

nodes.name: arch_diagram.py
nodes.summary: Renders an interactive and static terminal architecture diagram of the PolicyValueNet and SetupNet, showing layer shapes, parameter counts, and data flow using Rich Text box-drawing primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: This is an unusually large rendering module (1321 lines) built entirely from Rich Text primitives â€” no external diagram library. The ArchitectureDiagram class implements __rich_console__ for Live display integration.

nodes.name: ArchitectureDiagram
nodes.summary: Rich renderable class that implements __rich_console__ to output a scrollable, interactive terminal architecture diagram for the PolicyValueNet.

nodes.name: render_static
nodes.summary: Constructs a _StaticConfig and _StaticView from raw architecture parameters and returns the full list of diagram rows for non-interactive (static) rendering.

nodes.name: convergence.py
nodes.summary: Computes EWMA series and axis ranges for training convergence charts, including win-rate, score, and margin curves with regime-aware data point handling.

nodes.name: winrate_ewma_points
nodes.summary: Computes exponential moving average win-rate data points across training history, handling training regime transitions as separate EWMA series.

nodes.name: margin_ewma_points
nodes.summary: Computes EWMA data points for score margin history, handling regime transitions by splitting series at regime change boundaries.

nodes.name: setup_transition_iterations
nodes.summary: Identifies the training iteration indices at which the setup model phase transitioned, used to annotate convergence charts.

nodes.name: dashboard.py
nodes.summary: Implements the real-time terminal training dashboard using Rich layouts, rendering header stats, production metrics, learning charts, health indicators, and a pause/command panel.

nodes.name: build_layout
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the Rich Layout tree defining the training dashboard panels (header, produce, learning, headline, health, timer, events).

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Updates all layout panels in a single frame render, dispatching to each section's rendering function with the current RunState.

nodes.name: _health_rows
nodes.summary: Generates Rich text rows for the health panel by computing learning health metrics (loss, gradient, entropy) and performance stats.

nodes.name: _pause_panel
nodes.summary: Renders the interactive pause/command panel that displays key bindings, current command input buffer, and recent game statistics while the loop is paused.

nodes.name: loop.py
nodes.summary: Defines the TrainingLoop class orchestrating the RL self-play training cycle: game collection, model updates, evaluation, checkpointing, and system monitoring across all training phases.

nodes.name: TrainingLoop
nodes.summary: Main training loop controller that initializes network, optimizer, learner, and collectors, then drives the iteration loop with setup phase management, self-play collection, weight updates, evaluation, and checkpointing.

nodes.name: loop_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Handles iteration-level checkpointing and artifact persistence: saving model weights, game history logs, metrics, and writing final run summaries to disk atomically.

nodes.name: commit_iteration
nodes.summary: Commits all per-iteration artifacts (metrics, history, optional checkpoint) and updates run state progress after each training iteration.

nodes.name: checkpoint
nodes.summary: Writes a full checkpoint bundle including model weights, run state, metrics history, and HTML summary to the checkpoint directory.

nodes.name: loop_eval.py
nodes.summary: Manages periodic evaluation against a frozen opponent, opponent promotion logic (graduation from random phase, advancing to stronger opponent snapshots), and eval result recording.

nodes.name: maybe_evaluate
nodes.summary: Checks whether an eval is due, runs evaluate_vs_opponent against the current frozen opponent, and records the results into run state and the eval history log.

nodes.name: maybe_advance_opponent
nodes.summary: Promotes the frozen opponent to the most recent checkpoint when win rate exceeds the advancement threshold, loading the new opponent and recording the transition event.

nodes.name: loop_resume.py
nodes.summary: Handles training run resumption from checkpoints: loading saved weights and optimizer state, initializing phase, target, and history logs for fresh or resumed runs.

nodes.name: maybe_resume
nodes.summary: Loads a saved checkpoint if one exists and is architecture-compatible, restoring network weights, optimizer state, and run progress.

nodes.name: write_run_metadata
nodes.summary: Writes model config JSON, session record, and inspect report to disk at run start, capturing architecture and encoding parameters for the run.

nodes.name: loop_setup.py
nodes.summary: Manages setup phase logic for each training iteration: determining if setup phase is active, running offline setup network fitting, syncing embedder weights between main and setup nets, and resuming from setup checkpoints.

nodes.name: update_setup
nodes.summary: Runs a mini-batch training step on the setup network using accumulated setup phase game data, recording the resulting loss and accuracy metrics.

nodes.name: maybe_resume_setup
nodes.summary: Attempts to load a saved setup model checkpoint, checking architecture compatibility and restoring weights and optimizer state if found.

nodes.name: run_offline_setup_fit
nodes.summary: Runs multiple offline training epochs on the setup net using historical game data when no live setup games are available.

nodes.name: loop_target.py
nodes.summary: Handles the target iteration event: checking if a target iteration has been reached, pausing to query the user for a new target, and resuming the loop after the response.

nodes.name: handle_target_reached
nodes.summary: Pauses the training loop at a target iteration, renders a summary panel, solicits a new target iteration from the user via stdin, and updates run state with the new target.

nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Defines all Pydantic data models for training metrics: score breakdowns, family counts, eval results, EWMA accumulators, iteration metrics, game outcomes, and system stats.

nodes.name: IterationMetrics
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing all per-iteration training metrics including game outcomes, produce stats, setup stats, system stats, and timing.

nodes.name: ScoreBreakdown
nodes.summary: Pydantic model tracking score contributions by category (birds, eggs, food, cached food, bonus, end-of-round) with arithmetic operators for accumulation.

nodes.name: SystemStats
nodes.summary: Pydantic model recording per-sample system resource metrics including CPU, RAM, GPU utilization and memory usage.

nodes.name: metrics_log.py
nodes.summary: Reads and caches the per-iteration metrics log from disk (JSONL format), providing the iteration history as a list of IterationMetrics for chart and convergence computations.

nodes.name: read_iteration_history
nodes.summary: Reads the iteration metrics JSONL log from the run directory, caching results to avoid redundant disk reads on each dashboard refresh.

nodes.name: runmeta.py
nodes.summary: Manages run metadata persistence: writing and reading model configs, session records, inspect reports, and HTML model summaries; also provides layout and dimension reporting utilities used by the dashboard and arch diagram.

nodes.name: ModelConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model serializing the complete model configuration (architecture, encoding spec, version) to a JSON config artifact saved alongside each checkpoint.

nodes.name: build_model_summary_html
nodes.summary: Generates a full HTML report of the model architecture and parameter counts using torchinfo, converting the Rich text architecture diagram to an HTML page.

nodes.name: runstate.py
nodes.summary: Defines RunState â€” the central mutable state object for a training run â€” tracking game history, EWMA metrics, timing, eval history, events, and progress; includes Phase and TrainingPhase enums.

nodes.name: RunState
nodes.summary: Central training run state object accumulating game outcomes, compute stats, eval history, and timing; provides EWMA accessors and serialization to/from RunProgress for checkpoint persistence.

nodes.name: Phase
nodes.summary: StrEnum defining the high-level training phases (SETUP, COLLECT, EVAL, CHECKPOINT, etc.) used to annotate iteration history and dashboard displays.

nodes.name: RunProgress
nodes.summary: Pydantic model that is the serializable snapshot of RunState, persisted to checkpoint files to enable run resumption.

nodes.name: setup_runmeta.py
nodes.summary: Provides the SetupConfig Pydantic model and functions to write and read setup model configuration artifacts alongside setup checkpoints.

nodes.name: SetupConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing the setup model's architecture configuration (SetupArchitecture + SetupEncoding) serialized to disk for version-compatible checkpoint loading.

nodes.name: sysmon.py
nodes.summary: Implements SystemMonitor, a background sampler that periodically reads CPU, RAM, and GPU utilization metrics using psutil and pynvml for display in the training dashboard health panel.

nodes.name: SystemMonitor
nodes.summary: Samples CPU, RAM, and GPU resource metrics on demand, wrapping psutil and pynvml with graceful fallback when GPU is unavailable.

nodes.name: theme.py
nodes.summary: Provides color theming utilities for the training dashboard: lerp/gradient color generation, gradient-rendered text, and win-rate-dependent hero color selection.

nodes.name: version.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Version model and artifact compatibility logic: parsing version strings, checking artifact compatibility against MODEL_VERSION, and adapting old encoding specs via compat shims.

nodes.name: Version
nodes.summary: Pydantic model representing a semantic version (major.minor) for training artifact compatibility tracking.

nodes.name: check_artifact_compatible
nodes.summary: Raises IncompatibleArtifactError if the artifact's version is not compatible with MODEL_VERSION, enforcing the FRESH/REGIME versioning contract.

nodes.name: adapt_encoding_for_version
nodes.summary: Applies compat shims to an encoding spec loaded from an older artifact version, patching fields added in newer model versions to ensure backward compatibility.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the agents subpackage, re-exporting the primary agent constructors (random_agent and cli_agent) for use throughout the training and play pipelines.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the reference random agent â€” a factory returning a closure that satisfies the Agent protocol by sampling uniformly from the offered choices using a provided RNG.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a closure pattern to bind the RNG into the returned agent callable, matching the generic Agent protocol without subclassing.

nodes.name: random_agent
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a uniform-random agent closure bound to the given RNG, serving as the baseline reference agent for evaluations and opponent play.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Provides the CLI agent factory and its supporting formatting/input helpers, enabling a human player to interactively select choices at the terminal with rendered board state.

nodes.name: cli_agent
nodes.summary: Factory returning a CLI agent closure that presents decisions to a human player at the terminal, rendering board state and dispatching setup decisions to dedicated sub-handlers.

nodes.name: interactive.py
nodes.summary: Low-level interactive terminal UI library providing keyboard-driven multi-section selection forms with ANSI rendering, used by the CLI agent for setup card/food picking.
nodes.languageNotes: Platform-conditional keyboard reading: msvcrt on Windows and termios/tty on Unix, with automatic fallback when TTY is unavailable.

nodes.name: Section
nodes.summary: Data class describing a labeled group of selectable options within the interactive form, carrying mode (single/multi) and a required count constraint.

nodes.name: select_form
nodes.summary: Interactive terminal form that renders multi-section option lists and drives keyboard navigation, returning the user's selections when constraints are satisfied or falling back to line-input mode.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the engine subpackage, re-exporting the Engine class from the core module for use across training, evaluation, and play components.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the model subpackage, re-exporting the core PolicyValueNet and related types for use across the training pipeline.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the players subpackage, re-exporting the player factory, spec parser, and loaders that assemble agents for use in training and evaluation.

nodes.name: factory.py
nodes.summary: Constructs typed agent callables from PlayerSpec configurations, wiring together policy networks, setup models, and logging for use in training collection and self-play evaluation.

nodes.name: build_agent
nodes.summary: Dispatches on PlayerSpec kind to return the appropriate agent: cli_agent, random_agent, or a logged policy agent backed by loaded network weights.

nodes.name: resolve_split_setup_bonus
nodes.summary: Inspects a list of PlayerSpec configs and resolves whether any player uses a split setup bonus model, returning the relevant spec or None.

nodes.name: resolve_split_setup_food
nodes.summary: Inspects a list of PlayerSpec configs and resolves whether any player uses a split setup food model, returning the relevant spec or None.

nodes.name: spec.py
nodes.summary: Defines the PlayerKind enum and PlayerSpec Pydantic model, plus a parser that converts a raw string specification (random/cli/checkpoint path) into a typed PlayerSpec.

nodes.name: PlayerSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a player's kind, raw spec string, optional checkpoint path, and optional run directory for loading neural network weights.

nodes.name: batched_collect.py
nodes.summary: Implements batched inference self-play collection: a _BatchInferenceServer accumulates per-step inference requests from concurrent game threads and processes them as a single GPU batch, amortizing inference overhead.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses threading (Condition + Events) to synchronize game worker threads with the single inference server thread, forming a producer/consumer barrier.

nodes.name: _BatchInferenceServer
nodes.summary: Background server thread that batches incoming inference requests from concurrent game workers and dispatches them together through the policy network for throughput.

nodes.name: collect_games
nodes.summary: Runs waves of concurrent game threads against a shared batch inference server, collecting game records until all seeds are consumed or a stop condition fires.

nodes.name: collect.py
nodes.summary: Core single-process game collection logic: plays individual Wingspan games with optional setup-phase tree sampling, recording per-step state/choice/action tuples as training data.
nodes.languageNotes: Central module consumed by both single-process and multiprocess collection paths; defines GameRecord, SetupGameSpec, and the play_game/play_game_with_setup entry points.

nodes.name: GameRecord
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a completed game's per-step training tuples, per-player score breakdowns, winner index, random seed, and any setup phase samples.

nodes.name: play_game
nodes.summary: Plays a single Wingspan game, recording all per-step policy inputs and the chosen action indices for later REINFORCE/AC training.

nodes.name: play_game_with_setup
nodes.summary: Plays a game with the full setup-phase sampling pipeline, running the setup policy to choose initial cards and food before the main game begins, and recording setup samples for training.

nodes.name: evaluate.py
nodes.summary: Evaluation harness that runs a trained policy network against an opponent or in self-play, computing win margins and summary statistics for use in the training loop quality gate.

nodes.name: evaluate_vs_opponent
nodes.summary: Runs n_pairs evaluation games between two networks (each playing both seats), accumulates score margins, and returns a summary EvalResult.

nodes.name: run_final_self_play_eval
nodes.summary: Runs a comprehensive self-play evaluation suite tracking win rates, average scores, and per-category breakdowns across n_games games, producing FinalEvalStats.

nodes.name: learner.py
nodes.summary: Implements the REINFORCE/actor-critic gradient update step: flattens collected game records, bucketizes steps by choice count for efficient batched forward passes, and applies policy + value loss with gradient clipping.
nodes.languageNotes: Steps are grouped into buckets by their legal-action count so forward passes use uniform-size choice tensors, avoiding padding overhead for variable-length choice sets.

nodes.name: UpdateStats
nodes.summary: Pydantic model holding a single gradient-update result: per-component losses, entropy, gradient norm, advantage statistics, and step count.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Performs one full gradient update on the policy network from a batch of GameRecords: flattens steps, bucketizes by choice width, runs forward passes, computes combined policy/value loss, and clips gradients.

nodes.name: loop_collect.py
nodes.summary: Training-loop collection orchestrator that dispatches game collection to multiprocess or sequential modes depending on configuration, routing setup-phase and standard games and recording results into the training loop state.

nodes.name: collect_games
nodes.summary: Top-level per-iteration collection dispatcher: routes to setup-phase collection or direct multiprocess/batched game collection and records each completed game.

nodes.name: collect_with_setup_sequential
nodes.summary: Sequential (single-process) variant of setup collection: iterates over SetupGameSpecs, plays each game with setup-phase sampling, and records results directly.

nodes.name: loop_metrics.py
nodes.summary: Computes and assembles per-iteration training metrics from collected game records, including win rates, score breakdowns, family decision counts, and setup margins for dashboard reporting.

nodes.name: build_iteration_metrics
nodes.summary: Assembles a full IterationMetrics Pydantic object from collected records, update stats, eval result, and timing information for the current training iteration.

nodes.name: mp_collect.py
nodes.summary: Multiprocess game collection engine: a ProcessPoolExecutor-backed ProcessCollector spawns worker processes for self-play, setup-phase games, and evaluation, broadcasting updated weights via atomic temp-file saves.
nodes.languageNotes: Workers are initialized once per process (global state pattern) and reload weights lazily via version checks on the shared file, avoiding redundant weight transfers across games.

nodes.name: ProcessCollector
nodes.summary: Manages a ProcessPoolExecutor worker pool for parallel game collection and evaluation, handling weight broadcasting, task dispatch, and pool lifecycle.

nodes.name: policy.py
nodes.summary: Policy action-selection utilities wrapping the PolicyValueNet: provides sample_action (stochastic), greedy_action (argmax), greedy_agent (full agent closure), and raw logit/probability extraction functions.

nodes.name: sample_action
nodes.summary: Encodes state/choices, runs the policy network, and samples an action index proportionally from the resulting probability distribution using the provided RNG.

nodes.name: greedy_action
nodes.summary: Encodes state/choices, runs the policy network, and returns the argmax action index for deterministic greedy play during evaluation.

nodes.name: greedy_agent
nodes.summary: Factory returning a greedy (argmax) agent closure bound to the given network and device, used as the evaluation agent during self-play quality assessment.

nodes.name: policy_logits_and_probs
nodes.summary: Runs a single forward pass through the policy network and returns both the raw logits and the softmax probability distribution over legal choices.

nodes.name: steps.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Step Pydantic model, the atomic unit of training data capturing the encoded state vector, choice feature matrix, chosen action index, player identity, and decision family index for one agent decision.

nodes.name: Step
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a single training decision: encoded state array, choice feature matrix, chosen index, player id, and decision-family index for bucketed gradient updates.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the tournament subsystem, re-exporting key types and functions from app, config, participants, results, runner, schedule, and state modules.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for running a Wingspan AI tournament, supporting both live (rich dashboard) and quiet modes with result summarization and optional JSON report export.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines TournamentConfig, the Pydantic model holding all tournament settings including participants, ELO parameters, seeding, output path, and per-pair game count.

nodes.name: elo.py
nodes.summary: Self-contained ELO rating implementation providing EloTable (track and update ratings) and a replay function to re-compute ratings from a sequence of game results.

nodes.name: participants.py
nodes.summary: Defines ParticipantSpec/ParticipantKind/RunOption models and utilities for discovering, loading, and deduplicating tournament competitors from checkpoint directories.

nodes.name: picker.py
nodes.summary: Interactive terminal UI for selecting tournament participants from discovered training runs, with keyboard navigation, multi-select, and rich rendering.

nodes.name: results.py
nodes.summary: Defines result data models (GameResult, MatchupResult, ParticipantResult, TournamentReport) and the aggregate function that computes standings, matchup stats, and ELO from completed games.

nodes.name: runner.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates tournament execution by building a schedule, dispatching games either in-process or via a ProcessPoolExecutor, and collecting GameResult objects for aggregation.

nodes.name: schedule.py
nodes.summary: Generates a round-robin tournament schedule as a list of GameTask objects, assigning deterministic per-game seeds from a base seed, pair index, and round index.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Manages live tournament state for dashboard display, tracking ELO history, per-participant records, progress, throughput, and phase transitions during an ongoing run.

nodes.name: TournamentConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for tournament configuration with computed properties for participant IDs, pair count, and total games.

nodes.name: EloTable
nodes.summary: Tracks ELO ratings for a set of player IDs and provides expected score and update methods implementing the standard ELO formula.

nodes.name: replay
nodes.summary: Re-computes ELO ratings from scratch by replaying all game results in chronological order from an initial EloTable.

nodes.name: ParticipantSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic model describing a single tournament competitor with a unique ID, display name, kind (random or model), and optional checkpoint directory.

nodes.name: RunOption
nodes.summary: Represents a discovered training run as a participant option with metadata (iteration, win rate, modified date) and a to_spec conversion method.

nodes.name: load_player
nodes.summary: Loads a tournament competitor as a callable agent â€” either a random agent or a model-backed greedy policy agent loaded from the checkpoint directory.

nodes.name: discover_runs
nodes.summary: Scans a base directory for loadable training runs, returning a list of RunOption entries sorted by modification time.

nodes.name: run_picker
nodes.summary: Runs the interactive terminal competitor picker, returning the selected list of ParticipantSpec objects after the user confirms their selection via keyboard.

nodes.name: TournamentReport
nodes.summary: Top-level Pydantic model holding the complete tournament outcome: config, per-participant results, matchup breakdowns, and raw game records.

nodes.name: GameResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single completed game outcome including player IDs, scores, orientation, and start-player flag.

nodes.name: aggregate
nodes.summary: Aggregates raw game results into a TournamentReport by computing ELO ratings, per-pair matchup stats, and overall standings sorted by win rate.

nodes.name: run_tournament
nodes.summary: Entry point for running a full tournament: builds the schedule, dispatches games in-process or in parallel based on the in_process flag, and returns the aggregated TournamentReport.

nodes.name: play_tournament_game
nodes.summary: Plays a single tournament game between two agents resolved from specs and model caches, returning a GameResult with scores and player orientations.

nodes.name: GameTask
nodes.summary: Pydantic model representing a single scheduled game with round/pair indices, seat assignment, orientation, and deterministic deal seed.

nodes.name: build_schedule
nodes.summary: Generates the full round-robin tournament schedule as a flat list of GameTask objects, shuffling orientations per round with deterministic per-game seeds.

nodes.name: TournamentState
nodes.summary: Mutable live state object for an in-progress tournament tracking ELO history, per-participant records, events log, phase, timing, and standings computation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main entry point that resolves competitors, builds tournament config, validates loadability, then runs the tournament in live or quiet mode and writes the report.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines TrainConfig, the authoritative Pydantic model for all training hyperparameters covering the main network, setup model, ELO evaluation, encoding dims, and architecture key derivation.
nodes.languageNotes: TrainConfig is the single source of truth for architecture topology â€” its arch/setup_arch properties build ModelArchitecture/SetupArchitecture from the config fields, and architecture_key identifies encoding-shape changes for checkpoint compat.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the training configurator sub-package, re-exporting from controller, fields, runs, screen, and state modules.

nodes.name: controller.py
nodes.summary: Implements the input-handling controller for the interactive training configurator, dispatching keyboard events to navigate fields, edit values, confirm actions, and launch/archive training runs.

nodes.name: fields.py
nodes.summary: Defines the FieldSpec type hierarchy (IntField, FloatField, ChoiceField, LayersField, etc.) and all TrainConfig field descriptors, plus read/format/commit/nudge operations for the configurator UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines ~1100 lines of field metadata for every TrainConfig attribute, including visibility predicates (visible_when), grouping, help text, and step sizes â€” the single source of truth for the config form layout.

nodes.name: keys.py
nodes.summary: Cross-platform keyboard input abstraction providing KeyReader (context manager), KeyEvent/KeyKind models, and decode helpers for Windows msvcrt and POSIX termios input.

nodes.name: runs.py
nodes.summary: Provides RunSummary inspection, archive/clear operations, and status resolution for training run checkpoint directories, supporting the configurator's run management workflow.

nodes.name: screen.py
nodes.summary: Renders the full interactive training configuration screen using Rich, building the form panel, architecture summary, run info, field detail panel, and footer from ConfiguratorState.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Defines ConfiguratorState and related Pydantic models (ConfirmPrompt, ConfirmOption) representing the complete view-model for the training configurator TUI.

nodes.name: TrainConfig
nodes.summary: Master Pydantic config for the training pipeline with ~70 fields covering game collection, network topology, setup model, ELO evaluation, device, seeding, and architecture key computation.

nodes.name: KeyReader
nodes.summary: Context manager that puts the terminal into raw mode and provides a non-blocking poll() method returning KeyEvent objects, with platform-specific Windows (msvcrt) and POSIX (termios) backends.

nodes.name: RunSummary
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a snapshot of a training run directory: config, iteration, win rate, artifact presence flags, archive list, and modification time.

nodes.name: inspect_run
nodes.summary: Reads a checkpoint directory and populates a RunSummary by checking for artifact files and loading the last checkpoint payload if available.

nodes.name: archive_run
nodes.summary: Moves training run artifacts into a timestamped archive subdirectory, returning an ArchiveResult indicating which files were moved and any OS errors encountered.

nodes.name: run_configurator
nodes.summary: Entry point for the interactive training configurator: checks TTY availability, builds initial state, and runs the event loop, returning the final TrainConfig on exit.

nodes.name: dispatch
nodes.summary: Routes a keyboard event to the appropriate handler based on the current configurator mode (confirm, edit, or navigate).

nodes.name: build_initial_state
nodes.summary: Constructs the initial ConfiguratorState by inspecting the run directory and seeding the working config from any existing checkpoint.

nodes.name: FieldSpec
nodes.summary: Base Pydantic model for a TrainConfig field descriptor, carrying label, section, help text, unit, change impact, group, and optional visibility predicate.

nodes.name: editable_attrs
nodes.summary: Returns the ordered list of TrainConfig attribute names visible in the configurator form for a given config state, filtering by each field's visibility predicate.

nodes.name: commit
nodes.summary: Parses a raw string value for a field and validates it against the TrainConfig model, returning a validation error string on failure or None on success.

nodes.name: nudge
nodes.summary: Increments or decrements a field value by its step size (or cycles a choice field), updating the config in place with validation.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Assembles the full configurator terminal layout from state and frame dimensions, compositing header, form panel, arch panel, detail panel, run info, and footer.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for running Wingspan games, parsing command-line arguments, configuring players, initializing instrumentation, and executing one or more game loops.

nodes.name: main_play
nodes.summary: Main CLI handler that parses arguments, builds player agents, opens instrumentation, runs N games, prints results, and writes an optional log file.

nodes.name: _build_parser
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns the argparse.ArgumentParser with all CLI flags for game count, player specs, seed, device, and instrumentation options.

nodes.name: _open_instrumentation
nodes.summary: Reads an instrumentation config YAML file, resolves output directory, builds the Instrumentation object, and opens it with run context metadata.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the instrumentation subpackage, re-exporting the key public names (Instrumentation, config, events, handlers, registry) for convenient access.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Defines RunContext (run metadata Pydantic model) and InstrumentationConfig (YAML-driven config that validates handler assignments, resolves handler instances from the registry, and builds a live Instrumentation object).

nodes.name: RunContext
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing per-run metadata: output directory, run name, seed, player matchup, and worker ID, passed to handler open() calls.

nodes.name: InstrumentationConfig
nodes.summary: Pydantic model loaded from YAML that maps named handlers to event subscriptions, validates assignments against handler protocol capabilities, and builds a live Instrumentation dispatcher.

nodes.name: dispatcher.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Instrumentation class, the central event dispatcher that routes game lifecycle events to registered callback handlers, supporting open/close lifecycle and 14 distinct game-event methods.

nodes.name: Instrumentation
nodes.summary: Central event dispatcher for the Wingspan game engine: holds a mapping of EventName to handler lists, dispatches each game lifecycle event to registered handlers, and manages handler open/close lifecycle.

nodes.name: events.py
