nodes.summary: Defines EventName enum (14 game lifecycle events) and a Protocol class for each event type, establishing the typed callback interface that all instrumentation handlers must implement.

nodes.name: EventName
nodes.summary: StrEnum enumerating all 14 instrumentation event names (e.g., GAME_START, BIRD_PLACED, MADE_DECISION), used as keys when mapping handlers to subscriptions.

nodes.name: CallbackHandler
nodes.summary: Base Pydantic + Protocol class for all instrumentation handlers, providing default no-op open() and close() lifecycle methods that concrete handlers may override.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the handlers subpackage, importing card_visits and decision_logger submodules to trigger their registry self-registration side effects.

nodes.name: card_visits.py
nodes.summary: Implements CardVisitRecorder, an instrumentation handler that counts how many times each bird card is played across games and writes the results as JSONL to a configured output path.

nodes.name: CardVisitRecorder
nodes.summary: Handler that subscribes to bird_placed and game_end events, accumulates per-card placement counts, and flushes a JSON record per game to a JSONL output file.

nodes.name: decision_logger.py
nodes.summary: Implements DecisionLogger, an instrumentation handler that logs each agent decision (type, choice count, selected label) as a JSONL record to a configured output file.

nodes.name: DecisionLogger
nodes.summary: Handler that subscribes to the made_decision event and writes structured JSON records capturing the decision class name, number of choices, and the selected choice's display label.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Provides a name-keyed registry of instrumentation handler classes, exposing a @register decorator for handlers to self-register and lookup functions used by InstrumentationConfig to resolve handler types from YAML config.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Decorator factory that registers a CallbackHandler subclass under a given name in the module-level registry, raising ValueError on duplicate names.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Amazon Linux 2023 Docker image that installs CPU-only PyTorch and the wingspan package, then runs the cloud training entrypoint (`wingspan.cloud`) as an unprivileged user. Designed for CPU-only self-play training with artifacts shipped to S3.
nodes.languageNotes: Installs CPU-only torch via a dedicated index URL before the project install to avoid pulling the multi-GB CUDA wheel; runs under a fixed UID non-root user (flyway) for security.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context filter that restricts the image build to only `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`, and `src/`, explicitly excluding VCS metadata, virtual environments, checkpoints, tests, docs, and large artifact files to keep the image lean.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-specific guidance for AI coding agents, covering the worktree-based change workflow, architectural patterns to preserve (Decision/Choice system, Engine orchestrator, Agent protocol, bird power dispatcher), test conventions, and things to avoid. Serves as the authoritative contract between the codebase and any AI-assisted contributor.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary user-facing documentation covering setup, all CLI commands (play, dashboard, tournament, inspect, cloud, monitor), and pointers to deeper docs. The 8-section guide is the main entry point for new developers and users of the Wingspan simulator.

nodes.name: TODO.md
nodes.summary: Active task list tracking open work items across four areas: game-log display improvements for `wingspan play --log`, a training feature to bootstrap against a pre-trained opponent, a modeling change to use one-hots for round/cube counts, and general investigation topics requiring research reports.

nodes.name: cards.csv
nodes.summary: Master data table for all 180 Wingspan core-set bird cards, with one row per card capturing name, power text, point value, food costs by type, nest type flags, habitat flags, wingspan, egg limit, card color, and feature flags used by bonus cards and encoded as input features for the RL model.
nodes.languageNotes: 44-column CSV encoding a rich multi-dimensional card schema; the feature columns (px_*, bonus_*, flocking, predator, caches_food) are directly consumed by the encoder as binary and integer features.

nodes.name: coverage_baseline.txt
nodes.summary: Single-line file storing the current test coverage baseline percentage (85%), used by the quality gate script as a one-way ratchet â€” the gate fails if measured coverage drops below this value.

nodes.name: main.html
nodes.summary: Baseline HTML report for the neural network model architecture and parameter summary, generated by the `wingspan inspect` command. Presents layer-by-layer parameter counts, architecture diagrams, and decision-head tables in a styled single-page dashboard with interactive collapsible sections.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python package configuration defining the wingspan package (v0.1.0, Python 3.12+), runtime dependencies (PyTorch, Pydantic, rich, boto3), dev-only tooling (pytest, pyright, black, isort), the `wingspan` CLI entry point, and strict Pyright/black/isort/coverage settings used by the quality gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pyright strict mode with reportPrivateImportUsage=false to suppress torch stub noise; coverage configured for multiprocessing+thread concurrency to capture mp_collect worker output.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide (6 sections) for running Wingspan self-play training unattended in a container: describes the cloud architecture (S3-persisted, Spot-interruptible), image build steps, local smoke-test instructions, ECS Fargate Spot launch procedure, and the live monitoring command.

nodes.name: ecs-task-def.json
nodes.summary: Standalone AWS ECS Fargate task definition for the wingspan-trainer container: allocates 2 vCPU / 8 GB, wires execution and task IAM roles, routes logs to CloudWatch under /wingspan/trainer, and sets a 120-second stop timeout for graceful Spot-reclaim handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Template file â€” three placeholders (<ACCOUNT>, <REGION>) must be substituted before registering; designed as a Terraform-free alternative to main.tf for registering the task definition via the AWS CLI.

nodes.name: main.tf
nodes.summary: Terraform configuration that provisions the full durable AWS infrastructure for cloud training: an S3 bucket for run persistence, an ECS cluster with Fargate Spot as the default capacity provider, IAM execution and task roles scoped to the bucket prefix, a CloudWatch log group, and the ECS task definition for the trainer container.
nodes.languageNotes: Outputs cluster name, task family, and bucket name for scripted follow-on commands. The task definition's `command` is intentionally omitted so the per-run S3 config URI can be injected via `run-task --overrides` without re-registering the task definition.

nodes.name: aws_s3_bucket.runs
nodes.summary: S3 bucket that stores all training run artifacts: checkpoints, metrics, game logs, and status files, keyed under a configurable prefix.

nodes.name: aws_ecs_cluster.wingspan
nodes.summary: ECS cluster named 'wingspan' that hosts trainer tasks, configured to prefer Fargate Spot capacity for cost-efficient preemptible training.

nodes.name: aws_ecs_task_definition.trainer
nodes.summary: Fargate task definition for the wingspan-trainer container: 2 vCPU / 8 GB, awsvpc networking, 120-second stop timeout, CloudWatch log driver, wired to execution and task IAM roles.

nodes.name: aws_iam_role.task
nodes.summary: IAM role assumed by the running trainer container, granting scoped S3 GetObject, PutObject, and ListBucket permissions on the runs bucket.

nodes.name: aws_iam_role.task_execution
nodes.summary: ECS task execution IAM role granting Fargate the AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy permissions needed to pull the container image from ECR and ship logs to CloudWatch.

nodes.name: run.example.yaml
nodes.summary: Example cloud training run-file that configures a single named experiment: S3 bucket/prefix/region for persistence, sync cadences (status heartbeat, checkpoint upload, game-log chunking), and a full TrainConfig block with hyperparameters for a 5000-iteration self-play run.
nodes.languageNotes: The `train` block maps 1:1 onto wingspan.training.config.TrainConfig; omitted fields use TrainConfig defaults. No credentials are embedded â€” authentication is delegated to the AWS standard chain (IAM task role on Fargate, ~/.aws locally).

nodes.name: BIRDS.md
nodes.summary: Exhaustive reference for all 180 bird power implementations: maps each EffectKind variant through its parser matcher, engine handler, and any known deviations or gaps. Organized into 27 power categories (food, eggs, cards, tuck, predator, pink reactions, etc.) with per-bird call-out tables and cross-cutting conventions.

nodes.name: BONUSES.md
nodes.summary: Documents all 26 bonus cards and 16 end-of-round goals: acquisition rules, scoring formulas, state-encoding stripes, choice-encoding deltas, and groupings by category. Covers dynamic cards (Breeding Manager, Ecologist, Oologist, Visionary Leader) separately and notes known scoring gaps.

nodes.name: DECISIONS.md
nodes.summary: Defines the full Decision/Choice taxonomy for the RL agent: 13 decision families (MAIN_ACTION through SETUP), their choice-vector stripe layouts, ALL_DECISION_CLASSES ordering rules, and the optional-then-commit flow. Authoritative reference for adding or reordering decision points.
nodes.languageNotes: Describes a generic Decision[C: Choice] type parameterized over Choice subclasses; the ALL_DECISION_CLASSES list is a stable one-hot ordering whose position changes constitute a FRESH versioning event.

nodes.name: PROJECT.md
nodes.summary: Top-level package map for the Wingspan simulator: lists what is modelled (180 birds, 26 bonus cards, 16 goals), the root-level modules, and links to each subpackage's INDEX.md for detailed module listings.

nodes.name: QUALITY.md
nodes.summary: Reference for the quality_gate.sh CI script: describes the gate's phases (pyright, isort, black, pytest, coverage), exit codes (0 pass / 1 code problem / 2 infra failure), targeted section flags for partial runs, and the coverage ratchet mechanism.

nodes.name: RESEARCH.md
nodes.summary: Research agenda cataloguing planned analytical projects (card power rankings, model interpretation, score distribution analysis) with per-project feasibility verdicts and an infrastructure gap analysis updated June 2026. Identifies which projects are runnable today vs. need new harnesses.

nodes.name: TRAINING.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative training program document covering the full RL self-play pipeline: actor-critic algorithm selection, epoch sizing, throughput profiling (collection vs. update bottleneck), GPU memory optimization (padding elimination), checkpointing strategy, architecture scaling guidance, phased roadmap (Phase 0â€“3), and MLOps logging recommendations.
nodes.languageNotes: Details the 42x memory win from eliminating choice-set padding, the decoupled actor/learner design for parallelism, and the setup model's actor-critic mode with warmup schedule options.

nodes.name: VERSIONING.md
nodes.summary: Defines the artifact versioning contract for model checkpoints: FRESH vs. REGIME change taxonomy, MODEL_VERSION changelog (v0.0 through v0.2), compat shim rules, MINOR/MAJOR bump triggers, and the load+play guarantee that every versioned checkpoint must satisfy.

nodes.name: auto_merge_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Automated worktree merge loop that calls merge_worktree.sh repeatedly and invokes the Claude CLI to diagnose and fix quality-gate failures or git conflicts, retrying up to MAX_RETRIES times before giving up.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates AI-assisted repair to the 'claude -p' CLI, passing structured failure context via a mktemp prompt file to avoid quoting issues.

nodes.name: create_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Creates a named git worktree on a 'wt/<slug>' branch from the current HEAD of main, installs a fresh virtual environment inside the worktree, and writes a merge-authorization lock file to prevent premature merges.
nodes.languageNotes: Commits any dirty main state before branching (WIP snapshot) to ensure the worktree's base commit is clean; mirrors the main venv using pip install -e '.[dev]'.

nodes.name: merge_worktree.sh
nodes.summary: Squash-merges a completed feature worktree into main after verifying the merge-auth lock is absent; refreshes the main venv if pyproject.toml changed, runs the quality gate with coverage, commits, pushes, and cleans up the worktree and branch.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a precise exit-code protocol (0-5) to distinguish authorization blocks, git conflicts, gate failures, preflight errors, and infrastructure failures, enabling the auto_merge_worktree.sh retry loop to respond appropriately.

nodes.name: quality_gate.sh
nodes.summary: The single sanctioned quality gate for the project: runs strict pyright type-checking, isort + black formatting, a second pyright pass post-format, and pytest (parallel by default via pytest-xdist); optionally measures coverage and enforces a one-way ratchet against coverage_baseline.txt.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes exit code 1 (genuine check failure â€” fix the code) from exit code 2 (infrastructure/environment failure â€” stop and ask a human) so callers can automate retry logic without masking environment breakage.

nodes.name: infra_error
nodes.summary: Prints an environment-problem message to stderr and exits with exit code 2 (EXIT_INFRA), signalling to callers that the failure is not a code problem and must be resolved by a human.

nodes.name: usage
nodes.summary: Prints the script's header comment block as usage documentation by stripping the leading comment markers with awk.

nodes.name: header
nodes.summary: Prints a formatted section banner to stdout, used to visually delimit each gate step (pyright, isort, black, pytest, coverage) in the terminal output.

nodes.name: bonus.json
nodes.summary: Complete definition database for all 26 Wingspan bonus cards, including scoring conditions, VP thresholds, rulings, and expansion set membership. Each entry carries human-readable condition text, percentile statistics, and official FAQ rulings used by the engine's bonus scoring logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Records mix core and expansion cards (core, americas, asia, oceania, european); VP field encodes bracket thresholds as a formatted string (e.g. '2 to 3 birds: 3[point]; 4+ birds: 7[point]') that the parser must decode.

nodes.name: general.json
nodes.summary: Indexed collection of 34 official Wingspan game rulings and clarifications, each with a name, explanatory text, and source URL. Provides the authoritative text for edge-case mechanics such as pink power timing, end-of-round goal counting, and birdfeeder reroll rules used across the engine.

nodes.name: goals.json
nodes.summary: Full definitions for all 16 Wingspan round-goal tiles used in 2-player games, including goal text, expansion set, per-rank VP scores (ranks 1-4 for up to 4 players), reverse-side goal text, and numeric tile and goal IDs. Consumed by the engine's round-goal scoring and encoding layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Rank scores of -1 indicate a rank is unreachable at that player count; the set field identifies which expansion introduced each goal tile.

nodes.name: master.json
nodes.summary: Canonical database of all 180 Wingspan bird cards (plus expansion cards), containing every attribute the engine and encoder need: habitat eligibility, food costs, nest type, egg limit, wingspan, victory points, power text, color, bonus-card qualifiers, rulings, and set membership. This is the single source of truth for card definitions loaded by the card parser.
nodes.languageNotes: Each bird entry flags membership in all 26 bonus card categories (e.g. 'Anatomist', 'Cartographer') as null or 'X', enabling O(1) bonus scoring lookups. Power text is raw English prose that the card parser's regex matchers must parse into EffectKind variants.

nodes.name: parameters.json
nodes.summary: Minimal runtime display-parameter configuration with a single key controlling whether bonus-card match symbols are shown in the UI. Provides default values that can be overridden at runtime.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the v0.0 backwards-compatibility fixture set: a pinned snapshot of a real training run's artifacts (model and setup checkpoints plus JSON descriptors) used by test_compat_v0_0.py to verify that artifact version 0.0 files still load and run games under the current code. Explains the Git LFS storage strategy, the deliberate absence of a version key in the JSON descriptors (to exercise the default-to-0.0 code path), and the policy for capturing future fixture sets on MINOR version bumps.

nodes.name: model_config.json
nodes.summary: Pinned v0.0 main network architecture descriptor used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture. Captures the encoding dimensions (state_dim=771, choice_dim=397), the 12-family decision ordering, and the full ModelArchitecture topology (2-layer trunk/choice heads at width 128, card embedding dim 64, shared card encoder, distinct hand model, tray set embedding) as it existed before the version field was introduced.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately omits a version key to exercise the default-to-0.0 load path; do not add one.

nodes.name: setup_config.json
nodes.summary: Pinned v0.0 setup model configuration fixture recording the setup network architecture (setup_feature_dim=308, two-layer MLP at 128/64) plus the full main architecture descriptor used alongside the setup net. Used by test_compat_v0_0.py to confirm the setup checkpoint loads and plays correctly under current code.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately omits a version key to exercise the default-to-0.0 load path; do not add one.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the v0.1 backwards-compatibility fixture set: a pinned snapshot of a real training run captured when MODEL_VERSION was bumped to 0.1, used by tests/test_compat_v0_1.py to guarantee that v0.1 artifacts continue to load and play games under future code. Describes the capture methodology, Git LFS storage of compressed checkpoints, and the policy for retaining fixture sets across MINOR version bumps.

nodes.name: model_config.json
nodes.summary: Pinned v0.1 main network architecture configuration used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture. Captures the 12-family decision ordering, state_dim=771, choice_dim=215, and the full ModelArchitecture descriptor (trunk/choice/head layers, card embedding, distinct hand model, tray set embedding) that defines the v0.1 encoding contract.
nodes.languageNotes: Carries an explicit version: "0.1" field, exercising the stamped-version load path in the compat test suite.

nodes.name: setup_config.json
nodes.summary: Pinned v0.1 setup network configuration used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture alongside model_config.json. Records setup_feature_dim=308, the setup model's hidden-layer architecture, and an embedded copy of the main network architecture descriptor, all tagged with version: "0.1".
nodes.languageNotes: Contains both a setup_arch block (shallow MLP for the initial bird-selection phase) and a full main_arch block, reflecting the dual-model design at v0.1.

nodes.name: .gitattributes
nodes.summary: Git line-ending configuration ensuring shell scripts are always checked out with LF endings to prevent CRLF corruption when running quality gate scripts on Windows.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Exclusion patterns for the understand-anything analysis tool, listing generated directories, test artifacts, data files, and virtual environments to skip during codebase indexing.

nodes.name: export_card_attrs.py
nodes.summary: Developer script that exports all 180 core-set bird cards as CSV rows with 44 unnormalized attribute columns (food costs, nest type, habitats, exchange vectors), useful for external analysis and card power research.

nodes.name: _bird_row
nodes.summary: Builds a complete CSV row dict for a single bird card, assembling food costs, nest bits, habitat flags, exchange vector, and all other attributes in unnormalized form.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loads all core-set cards and writes one CSV row per bird to stdout or a named file, using DictWriter with the full attribute column list.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration providing test collection ordering (pool-based tests last) and a session-scoped fixture that resets PyTorch thread count after tests to avoid resource contention.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pytest hooks (pytest_collection_modifyitems) and a session-scoped autouse fixture to enforce consistent test execution order and thread cleanup.

nodes.name: test_compat_v0_1.py

nodes.name: pytest_collection_modifyitems

nodes.name: restore_torch_thread_cap

layers.name: Game Domain

layers.description: The Wingspan game simulation engine â€” Engine orchestrator, all main-action transitions, bird power handlers (brown/white/pink), reactor dispatch, scoring, and stateless validation helpers.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: README.md establishes what this project is: a complete 2-player Wingspan board-game simulator paired with a reinforcement learning self-play training pipeline. It documents all six CLI sub-commands (play, dashboard, tournament, inspect, cloud, monitor) and orients you to the two-part nature of the codebase â€” a faithful game engine and an RL research platform built on top of it. Reading this first sets the context for every architectural decision you'll encounter in the steps that follow.

tour.title: Package Map & Architecture Docs
tour.description: docs/PROJECT.md is the authoritative top-level package map, linking to per-subpackage INDEX files for each area of the codebase. Reading it alongside README gives you a concrete picture of how the source tree is organized before diving into any individual module. The eight architectural layers documented here â€” Game Domain, Game Engine, ML Encoding, RL Training Pipeline, Application Layer, Game Data, Infrastructure, and Documentation â€” form the structural backbone that the rest of this tour follows.

tour.title: CLI Entry Point
tour.description: src/wingspan/__main__.py is the single front door for all user-facing commands. It uses Python 3.12's match/case to dispatch six sub-commands â€” play, dashboard, tournament, inspect, cloud, and monitor â€” each wiring together the appropriate layers of the codebase. With the highest fan-out in the entire graph (7 direct imports), reading __main__.py gives you an immediate map of which modules matter at runtime and how the game-play, training, and evaluation paths diverge from a common entry point.
tour.languageLesson: Python 3.12's structural pattern matching (`match/case`) is ideal for command dispatchers: each `case` branch matches a string literal and can bind sub-expressions in one step, replacing long if/elif chains with a readable, exhaustive switch.

tour.title: Card Schema & Game Data
tour.description: cards/schema.py is the most imported file in the entire codebase (fan-in 10) â€” it defines the domain vocabulary every other module speaks. It houses the EffectKind StrEnum with 60+ variants that name every bird power in the game, plus the Bird, BonusCard, and RoundGoal types. master.json is the static database of 180+ bird records that the parser loads and maps to these types at startup; schema.py and master.json are a tightly-coupled cluster â€” the types and the data that populates them.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `StrEnum` (3.11+) lets enum members double as plain strings, so `EffectKind.GAIN_FOOD == 'gain_food'` is true. This makes JSON serialization and pattern-matching on raw strings seamless without a separate conversion step.

tour.title: Game State
tour.description: state.py defines GameState and PlayerState â€” the complete mutable snapshot of a Wingspan game at any moment. Every board slot, food token count, egg placement, birdfeeder die, and per-turn scratch field lives here. With a fan-in of 8, it is the second-most-depended-upon module after schema.py; nearly every module in the Game Engine layer reads or writes these objects. Understanding what GameState holds is prerequisite to understanding how the Engine drives it.

tour.title: Decisions & Choices
tour.description: decisions.py defines the entire decision-point vocabulary for the game (fan-in 9, second only to schema.py). It contains all 41 Choice subclasses â€” each a typed Pydantic model with named fields rather than opaque payload tuples â€” and 6 generic Decision[C] families. The module also exports ALL_DECISION_CLASSES, the stable one-hot ordering used by the RL encoder; the order of this list is append-only and is a versioning contract. docs/DECISIONS.md is the companion reference explaining the taxonomy, stripe layout, and every engine call site.
tour.languageLesson: The `Decision[C: Choice]` pattern uses Python 3.12 PEP 695 generic syntax to bind the type parameter at the class level. This lets the Engine's `ask()` method return the exact Choice subtype the caller expects â€” no casting, no Any â€” while accepting a single non-generic `list[Agent]` at use sites.

tour.title: Bird Powers Reference
tour.description: docs/BIRDS.md is the comprehensive reference mapping all 180 birds and 26 bonus cards to their EffectKind variants, handler functions, and implementation status. It is the essential lookup when tracing why a particular EffectKind exists in schema.py or finding the handler that processes a given bird's power. Studying it alongside the schema gives you a complete picture of what the game engine must simulate before you look at how the Engine orchestrates it.

tour.title: Game Engine Core
tour.description: engine/core.py is the orchestrator that owns the full game lifecycle â€” player setup, the four-round turn loop, the `ask()` method that validates agent responses against offered Choice sets, and end-game scoring. It is the structural hub of the Game Engine layer (fan-in 5, fan-out 7), delegating each phase to sibling free-function modules: actions.py for state transitions, reactors.py for pink powers, scoring.py for final tallying, and grants.py for activation effects. Understanding Engine.ask() is the key â€” it is the sole channel through which agents (human, random, or neural) interact with the simulation.

tour.title: Actions, Reactors & Scoring
tour.description: actions.py implements the five main-action state transitions (play bird, gain food, lay eggs, draw cards, end-of-round), while reactors.py orchestrates the pink between-turn powers by dispatching EffectKind events to the handler registry. grants.py is the largest single power-handler module, covering all grant effects triggered on bird activation. Together these three modules represent the full behavioral surface of the game engine; scoring.py closes the loop by computing final point totals and per-choice reward deltas that feed directly into the RL encoder.

tour.title: Agents & Player Factory
tour.description: agents/base.py provides the reference Agent implementation â€” random_agent â€” a one-function module that selects uniformly from the offered Choice set. The Agent protocol is structural (typing.Protocol), so any callable with the right signature qualifies. players/factory.py builds concrete agents from PlayerSpec configurations, wiring model weights, policy functions, and setup handlers together; it is the bridge between raw model checkpoints and a runnable agent the Engine can call. The factory is used by both the interactive play command and the evaluation loop in training.

tour.title: ML Encoding Layer
tour.description: The encoding layer translates game state and decision choices into fixed-size float32 vectors the neural network can process. encode/layout.py is the single source of truth for all vector dimension constants and stripe offsets (fan-in 3) â€” every encoder and the model itself imports it. state_encode.py converts the full GameState into an ~2381-dim observation vector covering board slots, food, birdfeeder, hand, and bonus/goal progress. choice_encode.py does the same for every Choice subtype. Together these three files form the interface contract between the game simulation and the PyTorch model.
tour.languageLesson: Centralizing all dimension constants in a single layout module prevents the silent mismatch bugs that arise when two modules independently compute 'how wide is the state vector'. Any change to encoding shape automatically propagates to every consumer through the shared constants.

tour.title: Neural Network & Architecture
tour.description: model/core.py implements PolicyValueNet â€” the PyTorch actor-critic model that ingests encoded game state and per-candidate choice vectors to emit policy logits and a scalar value estimate. architecture.py defines ModelArchitecture, the single topology descriptor that captures all shape-bearing hyperparameters and generates the version-stamped ShapeKey. version.py encodes the FRESH vs REGIME versioning contract: shape-changing modifications require a MODEL_VERSION bump plus a backward-compat shim in wingspan.compat, while weight-preserving hyperparameter changes do not.

tour.title: RL Training Pipeline
tour.description: training/loop.py is the central RL orchestrator (fan-in 4) â€” it sequences self-play collection, gradient updates, periodic evaluation, and checkpoint saves into a single training cycle. training/collect.py handles single-game self-play, recording the (state, choice, outcome) tuples that become training data. training/mp_collect.py scales this to multiple CPU processes via ProcessPoolExecutor with fp16 IPC compression and weight broadcasting, delivering roughly 5x throughput versus single-threaded collection. training/learner.py performs the actual gradient step, computing joint policy and value losses against the collected GameRecord batch.
tour.languageLesson: fp16 (half-precision float) IPC compression halves the size of weight tensors sent between processes. The main process serializes weights to fp16 bytes, workers deserialize and upcast locally for inference, avoiding both the GIL bottleneck and excessive inter-process data volume.

tour.title: Training Config, Evaluation & Dashboard
tour.description: training/config.py defines TrainConfig â€” the ~70-field master configuration Pydantic model covering network topology, collection settings, evaluation cadence, and checkpointing. training/evaluate.py runs head-to-head games between the current model and a reference opponent, computing win rate and score statistics that determine whether training is converging. The FLIGHT PLAN dashboard (training/dashboard.py) renders all of this as a live full-screen TUI using Rich Live, with convergence charts, system metrics, and the active TrainConfig. docs/TRAINING.md documents the Phase 0â€“3 roadmap and convergence criteria.

tour.title: Cloud Deployment & Infrastructure
tour.description: cloud/runner.py implements HeadlessRunner â€” the orchestration class that wraps TrainingLoop with S3 checkpoint sync and graceful signal handling for long-running cloud sessions. The Dockerfile packages the application into an Amazon Linux 2023 container, and deploy/main.tf is the Terraform root module that provisions the AWS ECS cluster and ECR registry where training containers run. pyproject.toml ties the whole project together: it declares the `wingspan` CLI entry point, strict Pyright settings, and the coverage baseline that the quality gate enforces.
tour.languageLesson: Terraform's ECS + ECR pattern follows a standard cloud ML training shape: ECR stores versioned container images built from the Dockerfile, ECS task definitions reference those images and mount S3-backed volumes for checkpoint persistence, and task role IAM policies grant the container write access to the checkpoint bucket.
